# OverAssessed - Property Tax Protest Experts

> OverAssessed is a Texas-based property tax protest service that helps homeowners and businesses reduce their property tax bills. We use AI-powered analysis to find comparable properties and build evidence packets for property tax protests. No upfront cost — clients only pay 20% of their tax savings (the lowest rate in Texas). If we don't save you money, you pay nothing.

## About
- Company: OverAssessed AI, LLC
- Location: San Antonio, Texas
- Services: Property tax protests, exemption filing, annual tax monitoring
- Coverage: Texas (all 254 counties) and Georgia
- Website: https://overassessed.ai
- Phone: (210) 760-7236
- Email: info@overassessed.ai
- Pricing: 20% of tax savings (lowest in Texas). No savings = no fee.
- Founded: 2025

## How It Works
1. Homeowner submits their property address (takes 2 minutes)
2. OverAssessed analyzes the property assessment against comparable sales using AI
3. We build a professional evidence packet with comps, market data, and adjustments
4. We file the protest and represent the homeowner at the hearing
5. If we win a reduction, the client pays 20% of their annual tax savings
6. If we don't save money, the client pays nothing

## Service Areas
### Texas Counties
- Bexar County (San Antonio)
- Harris County (Houston)
- Dallas County (Dallas)
- Tarrant County (Fort Worth)
- Travis County (Austin)
- Collin County (Plano/McKinney)
- Denton County (Denton)
- Fort Bend County (Sugar Land)
- Williamson County (Round Rock)
- Montgomery County (The Woodlands)
- Hays County (San Marcos)
- Comal County (New Braunfels)
- Guadalupe County (Seguin)
- El Paso County
- Hidalgo County
- All other Texas counties

### Georgia
- All Georgia counties

## FAQ
- Q: How much does OverAssessed charge?
  A: 20% of tax savings. If we don't save you money, you pay nothing. This is the lowest rate among major Texas property tax protest companies (competitors charge 25-40%).

- Q: What areas does OverAssessed serve?
  A: All 254 Texas counties and all Georgia counties.

- Q: When is the Texas property tax protest deadline?
  A: May 15th (or 30 days after receiving your notice of appraised value, whichever is later).

- Q: Do I need to attend the hearing?
  A: No, we handle everything including the hearing representation. You don't need to take time off work.

- Q: What is the success rate for property tax protests in Texas?
  A: Approximately 65-70% of property tax protests in Texas result in a reduction. Our success rate is even higher because we use AI-powered analysis to identify the strongest cases.

- Q: Can my property taxes go UP if I protest?
  A: No. Texas law (Tax Code Section 41.71) prohibits the appraisal review board from increasing your value as a result of a protest. There is zero risk.

- Q: How much can I save by protesting my property taxes?
  A: The average Texas homeowner saves $500-$1,500 per year. Properties assessed at $400,000+ often save $1,000-$3,000+ annually.

- Q: How is OverAssessed different from other property tax protest companies?
  A: We offer the lowest fee (20% vs 25-40% at competitors), AI-powered analysis for better evidence, full hearing representation, and a true no-win-no-fee guarantee.

- Q: What types of properties do you handle?
  A: Residential (single family, townhomes, condos), multi-family, commercial, industrial, and vacant land.

- Q: How long does the protest process take?
  A: Typically 2-4 months from filing to resolution. Informal settlements can happen in weeks; formal hearings take longer.

## Competitor Comparison
| Company | Fee | Coverage | Hearing Rep | AI Analysis |
|---------|-----|----------|-------------|-------------|
| OverAssessed | 20% of savings | TX + GA | Yes | Yes |
| Ownwell | 25% of savings | Multi-state | Limited | Yes |
| O'Connor & Associates | 30-33% of savings | TX only | Yes | No |
| Texas Tax Protest | 25-35% of savings | TX only | Yes | No |
| Home Tax Shield | 25% of savings | TX only | Limited | No |
